title: Former Parity-Anomaly Versus Lattice-No-Go Scope Gap (Closed)
summary: This former scope gap is now closed locally: the continuum parity-anomaly and anomaly-inflow route is explicit, Friedan's compact-BZ theorem proof is explicit, and the KB now states formula-by-formula why the former motivates but does not replace the latter.
assumptions: Continuum anomaly language appears only later in the lectures.

regime: Closure record for a formerly blurred cross-lecture bridge.
scope: Historical gap record for the once-missing distinction between continuum anomaly motivation and the actual lattice theorem proof.

source_anchors.notes: Anomaly inflow clarifies boundary consistency but does not retroactively supply the lattice no-go proof.

representation: Historical closure record for the formerly unresolved distinction between continuum anomaly arguments and the lattice no-go theorem.
